---
title: "A composable ecosystem for infectious disease modelling"
description: "Why we are building EpiAware, and where it is today."
author: "Sam Abbott"
date: "2026-07-10"
categories: [announcement]
---

EpiAware is a set of composable Julia packages for infectious disease modelling.
The approach builds on work in the epiforecasts corner of code — the modelling workflow
and the composability grant — that showed how interchangeable parts make robust
outbreak analysis workflows easier to build and maintain. EpiAware brings that
design to Julia, with composability as the principle from the start.

There is no single way to work with these tools. The [approaches](/approaches/)
pages document two starting points — composing distributions into event trees and
assembling a full model from parts — that happen to exist now; more will follow
as the ecosystem grows.

## Where it is today

The ecosystem is at an early stage.
[CensoredDistributions.jl](/packages/censoreddistributions.qmd) is the most
developed and usable today; the joint-modelling and composition packages are in
active development. Each package's page says how settled it is.

## Get involved

We are looking for collaborators — whether you contribute code, documentation,
examples, or a modelling problem that composable tools could help with. See
[Get involved](/get-involved.qmd), or start a conversation on the
[epinowcast forum](https://community.epinowcast.org/).
